name: Herring (SS-233)
events: 5 Jul 1942 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN) arrived at New London, Connecticut.
23 Jul 1942 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN) arrived at Newport, Rhode Island for special torpedo tests.
3 Oct 1942 Having completed a long period of torpedo testing, USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN), returned to New London, Connecticut from Newport, Rhode Island.

8 Nov 1942 While on her 1st war patrol USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson) torpedoed and sank the Vichy-French merchant Ville du Havre (5083 GRT) east of Casablanca, Morocco in position 33°34'N, 07°52'W.
25 Nov 1942 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson) ended her 1st war patrol at Roseneath, Scotland.

1 Jan 1943 In a signal timed 0656 A/1, the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) informed USS Shad (T/Cdr. E.J. MacGregor, 3rd, USN), USS Blackfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Davidson, USN), USS Herring (T/Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN), USS Barb (T/Cdr. J.R. Waterman, USN) and HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) of the following; Aircraft report one enemy merchant ship within 15 miles of position 45°45'N, 13°50'W, course 90°, speed 11 knots. Inward-bound blockade-runners are sometimes escorted by U-Boats.

In a signal timed 0719 A/1 the F.O.S. sent the following orders to the above listed submarines; My 0641 A/1. If not acting on aircraft homing signals submarines operate as follows; HrMs Dolfijn between parallels 46°20'N and 46°01'N. USS Blackfish between 46°01'N and 45°40'N. USS Herring between 45°40'N and 45°20'N. HMS Barb between 45°20'N and 45°01'N. All submarines making west as far as possible to ensure interception of the enemy in daylight.

In a signal timed 0909 A/1 the F.O.S. sent the following information to the submarines listed above; Estimated positions of our surface forces at 0800/1; 1) HMS Scylla (Capt. I.A.P. Macintyre, CBE, DSO, RN) in position 42°50'N, 16°01'W. Northbound convoy MKS 4 escorted by sloops and corvettes in position 45°40'N, 14°50'W. 2) HMS Scylla and three escorts of MKS 4 have been ordered to proceed to intercept and have been informed our submarines are expected to intercept east of 13°00'W. [The Senior Officer of the escort had detached two of the escorts instead of three, this were HMS Lulworth (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) C. Gwinner, DSO, RN) and HMCS Moosejaw (T/Lt. L.D. Quick, RCN).]

In a signal timed 1114 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following information the the submarines listed above; My 0909 A/1, Paragraph 2, position, course and speed of HMS Lulworth and HMCS Moosejaw is 45° 37'N, 14°01'W, 090°, 15 knots.

In a signal timed 1151 A/1, the following orders were sent to the submarines listed; Estimate longitude enemy at 1200 A/1 was 12°30'W. If to westward of enemy USS Blackfish, USS Herring and USS Barb sweep on course 90° at best convenient speed.

In a signal timed 1247 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following orders to the submarines listed; My 1151 A/1. USS Blackfish, USS Herring and USS Barb are to operate between limits of latitudes ordered in my signal timed 0719 A/1. Adjust course as necessary.

In a signal timed 1624 A/1, the following was sent by the F.O.S.; 1) Estimate Dolfijn's position at 2000 A/1 as being 46°05'N, 12°30'W. 2) Dolfijn is to proceed forthwith on route 'Red', diving by day, passing through position 'C' (42°59'N, 12°26'W at 0600 A/3.

In a signal timed 1714 A/1, the following information and orders were sent by the F.O.S.; Aircraft report position, course and speed of the enemy at 1634 A/1 as being 45°12'N, 10°48'W, 090°, 12 knots. USS Shad and HrMs Dolfijn proceed to intercept.
In a signal timed 1830 A/1 to the Admiralty, HMS Scylla reported to have sunk the inbound enemy blockade breaker.

In a signal timed 1923 A/1, the F.O.S. sent the following information / orders; 1) Enemy was sunk by HMS Scylla at 1830 A/1. 2) Submarines return forthwith to original patrol. USS Shad off Bilbao, USS Barb off Vigo, USS Blackfish in zone 'K 501' and USS Herring off Ferrol. (1)

10 Feb 1943 HMS Severn (Lt.Cdr. A.N.G. Campbell, RN) departed Portsmouth for Holy Loch. She was to make rendez-vous the next morning with HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) and the US submarines USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN) and USS Shad (Lt.Cdr. E.J. MacGregor III). During the passage from Portsmouth to the rendez-vous position HMS Severn was escorted by the Free French submarine chasers Chasseur 11 and Chasseur 15. (2)

29 Mar 1943 In a signal timed 2229 A/29, the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) informed USS Herring (T/Cdr. J. Corbus, USN), ORP Sokol (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) J.K.S. Koziolkowski, DSC) and HMS P 31 (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSC, RN) of the following. Patrol orders were also included to try to intercept the reported enemy merchant vessel; Aircraft report at 1650 A/29, one enemy merchant ship escorted by five destroyers, one of which is a 'Narvik'-class, in position 45°50'N, 06°22'W, course 250°, speed unknown but possibly 12 knots. Proceed to reach the following positions by daylight on the 30th; USS Herring 44°46'N, 11°50'W. ORP Sokol 44°14'N, 12°26'W. HMS Uproar 45°10'N, 12°50'W.

This referred to the Italian blockade breaker Himalaya (6240 GRT, built 1929) which was on passage from Bordeaux to Japan. At the time that she was sighted by the aircraft she was escorted by the torpedo boats T 23, T 2, T 12, T 18 and Falke.

At 2130 B/29, the escort left Himalaya and turned back. They arrived at La Pallice at 1130 B/29. Himalaya proceeded alone but apparently had no been given the proper sailing orders and was way off her expected position. At the same time the 8th Z-Flottille (Z 23, Z 24, Z 32 and Z 37) had sailed to meet the Italian blockade runner Pietro Orseolo (6344 GRT, built 1939) to escort her in. At 2150 B/30, Himalaya signalled that she had been sighted by enemy warships (apparently, she had not been informed of the sortie of the 8th Z-Flottille) in Quadrat BE 9688 (43°51'N, 12°15'W), mistaking the German destroyers for British. She began the process of scuttling, destroying her secret papers and abandoning ship. Fortunately, the error was soon realised and the crew returned aboard, the scuttling cancelled but without her code books, she was forced to turn back and arrived at La Pallice at 1330 B/2 April.
In an amplifying report timed 2318 A/29 the course of the enemy was given as 255°.
[Multiple enemy blockade breakers were on the move both westwards and eastwards though the area at this time though.] (3)
9 Aug 1943 After Herring had made 5 war patrols out of Britain, she returned to the United States on 26 July 1943. She departed New London for the Pacific on 9 August 1943.
10 Aug 1943 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for the Panama Canal Zone.
15 Nov 1943 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the East China Sea and the Yellow Sea.
14 Dec 1943 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Hakozaki Maru (3948 GRT) north-east of Shanghai, China in position 33°01'N, 124°01'E.

1 Jan 1944 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Johnson) torpedoed and sank the Japanese aircraft transport Nagoya Maru (6071 GRT, offsite link) some 220 miles south-south-west of Tokyo Bay in position 32°10'N, 138°37'E.
16 May 1944 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. D. Zabriskie, Jr.) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol of the Kuril Islands.
21 May 1944 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. D. Zabriskie, Jr.) topped up with fuel at Midway.

31 May 1944 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. D. Zabriskie, Jr.) and USS Barb rendezvous in Sea of Okhotsk some 150 miles west of Matsuwa Island, Kurils, to plan operations against Japanese shipping in the vicinity.

Later that day Herring attacked a convoy sinking the Japanese frigate Ishigaki (860 tons, offsite link) and the Japanese army cargo ship Hokuyo Maru (1590 GRT) west of Matsuwa Island in position 48°00'N, 153°00'E.

1 Jun 1944 USS Herring (Lt.Cdr. D. Zabriskie, Jr.) torpedoed and sank the Japanese troop transport Iwaki Maru (3124 GRT) and the Japanese merchant cargo ship Hiburi Maru (4366 GRT) off Matsuwa Island in position 48°00'N, 153°00'E. Unfortunately Herring is sunk by a Japanese shore battery after this attack.

1 Jun 2026 The Naval History and Heritage Command announces that the wreck of USS Herring has been located and identified off Matsuwa Island. The wreck was first located by a Russian team in 2017, with a 2022 expedition placing a commemorative plaque on the site.
